formula_note: Direct use of HRW minimum confirmed armed-Palestinian deaths in Jenin.
uncertainty_note: Potential understatement if some unresolved or unrecovered cases were combatants; no upward adjustment applied.
quality_note: Strong source support from HRW field investigation.

formula_note: Direct use of HRW minimum confirmed civilian deaths in Jenin.
uncertainty_note: Minimum confirmed civilian count; some unresolved cases could increase this in sensitivity analysis.
quality_note: Strong source support from HRW field investigation.

formula_note: Explicit unresolved-status deaths retained outside the core DR path.
uncertainty_note: Sensitivity cases can place all 3 with civilians or all 3 with combatants; base estimate excludes them from both denominators and numerators in the DR.

formula_note: 9-day exposure window. Low=80 fighters x 9/30.4375; best=100 x 9/30.4375; high=300 x 9/30.4375. The 300 upper bound is a conservative conversion of the UN report's qualitative phrase 'hundreds of gunmen'.
uncertainty_note: Weakest component of the bundle. HRW gives 80-100 as media estimates; the UN report preserves an Israeli account of 'hundreds of gunmen'. The estimate therefore uses a deliberately wide range.
quality_note: Transparent but assumption-heavy denominator; acceptable only because the episode is tightly bounded.

formula_note: 9-day exposure window. Low=4,000 civilians remaining throughout x 9/30.4375; best=8,000 average civilians exposed; high=14,000 pre-battle camp civilians x 9/30.4375.
uncertainty_note: Civilian presence changed rapidly because many fled before or during the battle. The best estimate is intentionally a mid-scenario rather than a claim that 8,000 remained at every moment.
quality_note: Better grounded than militant exposure because both a pre-battle ceiling and a low-end remained-in-camp figure are source-supported.

formula_note: dr_v1_direct = (mil_deaths_direct / mil_person_months) / (civ_deaths_direct / civ_person_months). Low uses 27/300 fighters and 22/4,000 civilians over 9 days; best uses 27/100 and 22/8,000; high uses 27/80 and 22/14,000. The duration cancels algebraically because both denominators use the same 9-day window.
uncertainty_note: Very wide range by design. This is a side-specific Palestinian-victim DR for the Jenin camp battle, not a whole-Intifada or all-sides blended DR. Unknown-status Palestinian deaths are excluded under the strict rule; if all 3 unknowns were civilians the best-case DR would fall to 86.4, and if all 3 were combatants it would rise to 109.09.
quality_note: Numerators are strong; denominator uncertainty, especially militant exposure, dominates the interval.

formula_note: Separate side-specific military death series for Israeli forces in Jenin.
uncertainty_note: Recorded for completeness and side attribution, but not folded into the Palestinian-side DR because no comparable Israeli civilian-exposure denominator exists within the camp footprint.
